sections.content:
World model: Two dots painted: blue, light green. Remaining: dot 3 needs yellow, dot 4 needs purple. Palette shifted: gray, purple, gray, yellow. 
Plan: Click yellow square, then click dot 3 (col 34). Then click purple, then click dot 4 (col 40).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Find current palette squares
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

palette = []
for n in nodes:
    b = n['boundary']
    if 55 <= b[0][0] <= 62:
        palette.append((n['id'], n['color'], b[0][1]))

print("Bottom palette (sorted by col):")
for pid, color, col in sorted(palette, key=lambda x: x[2]):
    print(f"  Node {pid}: color={color}, col={col}")

# Find remaining gray dots
dots = []
for n in nodes:
    b = n['boundary']
    if 25 <= b[0][0] <= 35 and n['color'] == 'g':
        dots.append((n['id'], n['color'], b[0][1]))

print("\nGray dots (sorted by col):")
for did, color, col in sorted(dots, key=lambda x: x[2]):
    print(f"  Node {did}: color={color}, col={col}")

# Yellow square seems to be at col 42-45, center ~ col 43
# Dot 3 at col 34, center ~ col 34
# Dot 4 at col 40, center ~ col 40
# Purple square at col 26-29, center ~ col 27

action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 43},  # yellow
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 29, 'col': 34},  # dot 3
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 27},  # purple
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 29, 'col': 40},  # dot 4
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
